mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and declines in the federal funds rate signal central bank accommodation of inflation, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or interaction effect IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 > 2.0% and HOUST > 500k, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 55%+ of instances with INDPRO increases, or IC < 0.04 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong residential sales (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a housing market boom, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 55%+ of instances where RSAFS > 375k and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 8.5% signals improving labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE < 8.5%, or IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates expanding economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 55%+ of instances with GDPC1 growth, or IC < 0.04 in 2-year rolling windows.
